title: Harkov
content:

{{Quote|I have come with an offer for the Rebellion…The fleet under my command is willing to join the Rebel Alliance, for a price.|Harkov, speaking to Mon Mothma|audio=Harkov1.flac|<ref name="TF" />}}
'''Harkov''' was an [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] who served in the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] and before his defection in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Shortly after the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] victory in the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], he was charged with bringing an end to the [[Sepan Civil War]], and succeeded in bringing order to the [[Sepan system/Legends|system]]. However, during the campaign, he also supplied arms to both sides. Around the same time, Harkov began negotiating defecting to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], promising to take the [[Admiral Harkov's fleet|fleet]] under his command with him for a high enough price. Though Harkov took measures to cover his tracks, going so far as to kill Admiral [[Mordon (Human)|Mordon]] when the Imperial began to get suspicious, details of his dealings eventually came to light, and Imperial investigators began to close in on the admiral, forcing him to complete his defection ahead of schedule. However, Harkov was frustrated in his attempt to defect by the [[Prophets of the Dark Side|Secret Order of the Emperor]] and Imperial forces under the command of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Despite the assistance of his rebel allies, the Empire eventually captured him, and he was interrogated and executed by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]].

==Biography==
===Imperial officer===
Harkov appeared to be like any other [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], commanding the [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Protector (Victory I-class)|''Protector'']] with a small [[Admiral Harkov's fleet|fleet]] under his command, including the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Akaga]]''. However, all was not as it seemed.<ref name="TF" /> By [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="3ABY" /> the admiral had begun to take chances, selling Imperial weapons and equipment to any groups willing to pay. He also began to contemplate defecting to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] provided he could get a suitably high price for his services.<ref name="TF" />

[[File:Harkov3.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Harkov's shuttle travels to a meeting with the Rebel Alliance.]]
Harkov was almost exposed when a leak allowed Admiral [[Mordon (Human)|Mordon]] to learn of his potential treachery. Harkov was aware that, if news of his deceit and possible defection were to get out, the Empire's retribution would be swift and deadly, so he moved quickly against Mordon and had the admiral murdered. An investigation into Mordon's [[Death/Legends|death]] was launched, but the investigators were unable to identify the culprit, and Mordon's death was officially declared to be due to natural causes.<ref name="TF:TOSG">''[[TIE Fighter: The Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref>

Shortly after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], Harkov had a secret rendezvous with leaders of the Alliance, including [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]], and announced his intention to defect, promising that his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] would defect with him. Though the Rebels distrusted him, their need for experienced officers made them willing to take the risk, and they agreed to enter negotiations over his price.<ref name="TF" />

===Sepan Civil War===
{{Quote|The civil war that has ravaged your planets is over. It is time to lay down your arms. Join us, to rebuild all that was destroyed in this savage conflict. Even now, the process of repairing your planets has begun.|Harkov addresses the Sepan leaders|audio=HarkovSpeech-TF.flac|<ref name="TF" />}}
[[File:Harkov.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Harkov meets with Mon Mothma.]]
One of his last assignments as an Imperial admiral was to pacify the [[Sepan system/Legends|Sepan system]], which was in the grips of a [[Sepan Civil War|civil war]] between the inhabitants of [[Dimok/Legends|Dimok]] and [[Ripoblus/Legends|Ripoblus]], and to prevent the conflict from spreading to neighboring [[Star system/Legends|systems]]. The Empire was unaware at the time that Harkov had been supplying both sides in the war with weapons and equipment, but conflicting reports led the [[Prophets of the Dark Side|Secret Order of the Emperor]] to begin to question his handling of the situation.<ref name="TF" />

An hour after Harkov briefed his crew on the forthcoming campaign, the ''Protector'' jumped to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and arrived in the Sepan system in time to stop a [[Dimok (people)|Dimok]] [[First Battle of the Sepan system|attack]] on a [[Ripoblus (people)|Ripoblus]] convoy.<ref name="TF:TOSG" /> During the mission, [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]], a newly transferred [[TIE pilot]] acting on orders from the Secret Order, discovered Imperial armaments aboard one of the [[Starship/Legends|ships]] in the Ripoblus convoy, and ships were quickly launched to retrieve the weapons. Soon after, the ''Protector'' moved to stop a Ripoblus [[Battle of Science Station Youst|reprisal raid]] on the Dimok [[Scientist/Legends|scientific]] research installation [[Science Station Youst|Youst]]. Again, the Secret Order instructed Stele to inspect the facility, leading to the discovery and subsequent recovery of further weapons.<ref name="TF" /> Both discoveries threatened to lead the Secret Order back to Harkov, and the admiral warned Stele not to exceed his orders in the future.<ref name="TF:TOSG" />

Harkov next led the ''Protector'' into action to [[Second battle of the Sepan system|rescue]] Ripoblus scientists who were fleeing the war aboard the [[IPV-1 System Patrol Craft]] ''[[Glas]]'' and seeking asylum. The Imperial forces had to fend off attacks from both Dimok and Ripoblus forces to secure the safety of the refugees. Following this, [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] learned of a rendezvous between the Dimok and Ripoblus leaders near [[Sepan 8]] as the two factions looked to unite against the Empire, and the Imperials mounted an [[Battle of Sepan 8|attack]] to capture them. The operation was a success, but as it turned out, the ships at the meeting were actually carrying the children of the Dimok and Ripoblus leaders, intended to be used as hostages to ensure that each side complied with their new treaty.<ref name="TF" />

During the mission to capture the Sepan leaders, Stele located additional Imperial equipment aboard the [[cargo ferry]] ''[[Godendag]]'' and the [[CR92a Assassin-class corvette|''Assassin''-class corvette]] ''[[Runka]]'', further evidence that somebody was selling arms to both sides.<ref name="TF" /> After the battle, Harkov was contacted on a priority [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] channel as the investigation into who was selling Imperial equipment continued. Discrepancies in the ''Protector''{{'s}} supply manifests had led the investigators to Harkov's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], but Harkov attempted to deflect the investigators' questions to the ship's [[Quartermaster/Legends|quartermaster]].<ref name="TF:TOSG" />

With both sides united against the Empire, the Imperials had no choice other than to force a resolution. To this end, the ''Protector'' was to take a consignment of new [[TIE/ad starfighter|TIE Avenger]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] to replace the [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]] and [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE Interceptors]] lost in the previous missions. The ''Protector'' traveled to [[Gerbaud 2]] to rendezvous with the [[Ton-Falk-class escort carrier|''Ton-Falk''-class escort carrier]] ''[[Tropsobor]]''. With an [[Raid on the Tropsobor|attack]] from Dimok and Ripoblus forces expected, several [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] were to fly cover for the transfer. The Secret Order had reason to suspect that another ship would be coming to the rendezvous location, and instructed Stele to watch for anything unusual. Shortly after the enemy attack was repelled and the transfer was completed, Stele inspected the incoming [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] ''[[Omlaut]]'' to discover that it carried a high-ranking member of the Rebel Alliance. Harkov immediately disciplined Stele for his apparent inability to stick to orders and warned that his curiosity might get him into trouble.<ref name="TF" />

[[File:Protector_TIE.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Harkov's flagship, the ''Protector'']]
The ''Omlaut'' docked with the ''Protector'', and the Rebel was taken immediately to Harkov's private ready room for a meeting with the admiral. The Secret Order concluded that Harkov was responsible for the chronic shortages of supplies and had been attempting to cover his [[Black market/Legends|black-market]] dealings with both sides in the Sepan Civil War and, apparently, the Rebel Alliance. A member of the Secret Order later presented their suspicions to [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], who instructed them to bring him evidence before he moved against Harkov.<ref name="TF:TOSG" />

After the Imperials inflicted heavy losses on both sides in the Sepan Civil War, the two factions had little choice other than to accept peace under Imperial rule. Leaders of both worlds were brought to the ''Protector'', where Harkov addressed them, declaring the war over and promising that cooperation with the Empire would bring peace and prosperity.<ref name="TF" /><ref name="TF:TOSG" />

===Defection===
{{Quote|It means we're at war with the Empire. And it means we didn't get anything from the cursed Alliance!|Harkov, after his treachery is exposed|<ref name="TF:TOSG" />}}
Shortly after the end of the Sepan Civil War, the Secret Order had Stele transferred away from Harkov. However, as they closed the net on Harkov, they later had Stele transferred back to the ''Protector'' to assist them. A few days after Stele's arrival, Harkov met with his closest advisors. He feared that Stele may have been a [[Spy/Legends|spy]] for [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], and that this could have compromised his plans to defect to the Alliance, which he expected to be completed within a few days. [[Vondruln]], one of Harkov's advisors, was instructed to ensure that Stele was eliminated and to make it look like an accident.<ref name="TF:TOSG" />

Stele was instructed to show two rookie pilots how to clear a [[Space mine/Legends|minefield]] in an [[Deflector shield/Legends|unshielded craft]]. As Stele set about clearing the minefield, Harkov personally gave the order for his [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmen]], actually experienced pilots, to [[Battle of the minefield|eliminate]] him. However, the Secret Order had anticipated Harkov's actions, and had arranged for the [[Nebulon-B2 frigate]] ''[[Osprey]]'' to come to Stele's rescue. As Stele fled towards the ''Osprey'', the Rebel [[MC40a light cruiser/Legends|MC40a light cruiser]] [[Margaret (starship)|''Margaret'']] arrived to rendezvous with the ''Protector'', which quickly dispatched a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] carrying one of Harkov's officers. Stele was able to avoid pursuing TIE Avengers and Rebel [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]], and escaped on the ''Osprey''.<ref name="TF:TOSG" />

Harkov was furious about Stele's escape. His plan had been exposed, and he was now at war with the Empire, leaving him with no choice other than to join the Alliance immediately, and unable to get even a [[Decicred/Legends|decicred]] for his defection. Harkov summarily executed Vondruln for his failure.<ref name="TF:TOSG" /> With the ''Protector'' now dependent on the Rebels for supplies, Harkov fled to the Rebel supply outpost [[DS 5]] in the [[Parmel system/Legends|Parmel system]].<ref name="TF" />

Imperial [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|Assault Gunboat]] recon units were quickly dispatched to investigate several possible locations for the Rebel base in an effort to locate Harkov. The Emperor had dispatched [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Lord]] Vader to interrogate and execute Harkov, so the recon units were under instructions to locate him and report back. They were not to engage Harkov's forces, though they were to destroy any Rebel supply stores they encountered. It was Stele's recon unit that eventually located DS 5, and with it the ''Protector''. The Imperials proceeded to cut off Harkov's supply lines, initially at the [[Space station/Legends|station]], by [[Raid near DS 5|destroying]] the [[Cargo container/Legends|cargo containers]].<ref name="TF" />

Within an hour of the recon flight's return, the Imperials launched again and [[Attack on the DS 5 convoy|destroyed]] a supply convoy, headed for DS 5, consisting of [[BFF-1 bulk freighter/Legends|BFF-1 bulk freighter]] group ''[[Karflo]]'', the [[modular conveyor]] group [[SoroSuub (CNVYR)|''SoroSuub'']], the two [[container transport]]s ''[[Morgath 1]]'' and ''[[Morgath 5]]'' and the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] group ''[[Sundog]]''. With Harkov cut off from supplies, he was unable to conduct even limited operations, and the ''Protector'' was effectively stranded. However, Harkov was not given time to think and adjust his plans, as the Empire launched another quick [[Battle at DS 5|attack]], weakening his starfighter corps with their own TIE Avengers.<ref name="TF:TOSG" />

The Empire planned an assault to board and capture the ''Protector''. Harkov anticipated this attack and attempted to throw them off his scent by abandoning ship and taking refuge on DS 5, ordering the ''Protector'' to jump into hyperspace. However, during the attack on his starfighters, Stele inspected Harkov's shuttle, the ''[[Toten]]'', and discovered the admiral's subterfuge.<ref name="TF" />

===Capture===
{{Quote|Welcome, Admiral Harkov. We have a matter to discuss. What is the location of your fleet?…Now, you shall pay for your treachery.|Darth Vader, during Harkov's interrogation|audio=HarkovPays-TF.flac|<ref name="TF" />}}
[[File:Harkov2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Harkov is taken before Darth Vader.]]
With Harkov's location exposed, the Empire prevented his escape by using [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droids]] to watch for his escape while they planned their final attack.<ref name="TF:TOSG" /> The Rebels quickly laid a minefield and called for assistance from the [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] ''[[Olinor]]''. However, before the frigate arrived, the Empire launched their [[Capture of DS 5|attack]]. Assault Gunboats fought defending starfighters, while [[Gamma-class ATR-6 assault transport|''Gamma''-class ATR-6 assault transports]] moved against the platform itself. Harkov made one final attempt to flee aboard the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] ''[[Geddawai]]'', but his ship was disabled by Maarek Stele.<ref name="TF" />

After [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Vader arrived in the system aboard the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Garrett (Star Destroyer)|''Garrett'']], the ''Geddawai'' was boarded and captured by a [[Delta-class DX-9 stormtrooper transport|stormtrooper transport]]. Under [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] guard, the rebel was taken to the ''Garrett'' and brought before the waiting [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]]. Bitter and defiant, Harkov attempted to resist the Dark Lord, refusing to answer any questions during his short interrogation. He was soon punished for his defiance as Vader used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to [[Telekinesis/Legends|lift]] Harkov into the air and crush him.<ref name="TF" /><ref name="TF:TOSG" />

===Legacy===
Though Harkov was dead, the ''Protector'' and the ''Akaga'' were, with the help of Alliance forces, able to evade Imperial forces until, trapped by the [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruiser]] ''[[Harpax]]'', they were finally destroyed by the ''Garrett''{{'s}} forces. However, during this campaign, another high-ranking Imperial officer turned on the Empire, as [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Demetrius Zaarin]] used the distraction to mount a [[Zaarin insurrection|coup]] against the Emperor.<ref name="TF" />

==Personality and traits==
[[File:Harkov-ForceCrush-TIEFighter-Cutscene.png|thumb|left|250px|Darth Vader kills Harkov.]]
Harkov was a thin man whom, upon first seeing him, Maarek Stele considered to look sleep-deprived and erratic. He had a strong voice that carried his authority as a senior Imperial officer, but Stele found it edgy and his accent unpleasant. Harkov seemed to care little for any cause, and was willing to sell equipment to anybody willing to pay for it. Likewise, his attempted defection to the Rebel Alliance was motivated by desire for profit rather than any belief in the Rebel ideals.<ref name="TF:TOSG" />

He could also be a difficult commander to work under. He maintained a high level of security and ran his ship very strictly, discouraging his subordinates from acting outside of their orders or asking unnecessary questions, all of which led to low morale on board. In one instance, when [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Hamo Blastwell|Trace]] made a joke during a briefing by Harkov, the admiral struck him. Trace would later advise Stele that he would be better off if he avoided the admiral's attention.<ref name="TF:TOSG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Harkov was created for the [[1994]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']], in which he was voiced by [[Nick Jameson]]. With the player in the role of Maarek Stele, Harkov features heavily in the early portions of the game, first as the player's commanding officer, and later as the main antagonist.<ref name="TF" /> In an interview in [[The Lucasfilm Fan Club Magazine 22|''The Lucasfilm Fan Club Magazine'' 22]], [[Lawrence Holland]], project leader and designer of ''TIE Fighter'', explained that the events of the Sepan Civil War were conceived to show the Empire at its best, stopping interplanetary disputes and establishing the infrastructure that would lead to prosperity. Harkov was intended to be the antithesis of this and abuses his position to prolong the war and profit at the expense of others.<ref name="Insider22">{{LucasFanClubCite|22|The Dark Side Illuminated}}</ref> The character's story was later expanded in ''[[TIE Fighter: The Official Strategy Guide]]'', by [[Rusel DeMaria]].<ref name="TF:TOSG" />

===Harkov's Defection===
{{Quote|Lord Vader has been foolish to underestimate me. He shall pay. My defection to the Rebellion will devastate the Empire.|Harkov|<ref name="GB">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]''</ref>}}
[[File:DarccYittreasHarkov-SWGB.png|thumb|right|Harkov]]
Following the release of ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' in [[2001]],<ref name="GB 1">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''</ref> LucasArts ran a competition to design a level for the game. The "Harkov's Defection" scenario by [[Antonio Cusano]] was one of the grand-prize-winning levels, and it, along with the other winning levels, was included with the ''Clone Campaigns'' expansion pack and made available for download on the LucasArts website.<ref name="BG downloads">{{LucasArts|url=products/battlegrounds/downloads.htm|text=Star Wars Galactic Battlegrounds}}</ref><ref name="BG clone">{{LucasArts|url=products/battlegrounds/clonecampaigns|text=''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns''}}</ref> The scenario expanded on Harkov's meeting with Mon Mothma, during which he first discussed his defection. However, the scenario shows Darth Vader knowing of Harkov's defection from the start, contradicting events as portrayed in ''TIE Fighter'' and ''TIE Fighter: The Official Strategy Guide''. In the scenario, Harkov is depicted with the same image used for fellow Imperial officer [[Hindane Darcc/Legends|Hindane Darcc]].<ref name="GB" />

Harkov starts the scenario with only two escorts and must reach Mon Mothma's base safely, fighting his way through Imperial forces, including [[Dark Trooper/Legends|dark troopers]] and [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentices]], sent by Vader to capture him. Harkov soon finds an abandoned transmitter, which he uses to signal the Alliance base. However, an Imperial attack has destroyed the bridge leading to the base, so Harkov, joining forces with [[Rebel trooper/Legends|Rebel troops]], must take the longer path through the forest. Along the way, he discovers two Imperial [[Prison/Legends|prisons]] that can be raided to rescue some Rebel [[Troop/Legends|troops]] and a captive [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Harkov's forces eventually reach a small Rebel outpost, only to find it has been decimated by an Imperial attack. Proceeding to Mon Mothma's main base, Harkov can locate and rescue several prisoners from the Rebel outpost and must fight his way past a pair of [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transports]] with the help of Rebel [[T-47 airspeeder/Legends|T-47 airspeeders]]. After destroying the Imperials' own camp, Harkov is finally able to reach Mon Mothma and begin negotiations while the furious Vader vows revenge.<ref name="GB" />
